data.task:
ATOM 0 of the machine-local cache retention priority — build the PURE, NON-DESTRUCTIVE retention-selection spine plus config. NO filesystem deletion, NO SQLite mutation, NO daemon wiring in this atom. This is the decision/schema layer everything else builds on; it must be unable to harm any store even if buggy.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. New module `packages/core/src/runner/retention.ts` (co-located with run-dir.ts, the sole owner of the machine-local run layout). It must be PURE: no imports of node:fs, node:sqlite, or the store — it only computes a decision from plain inputs.
   Export:
   - `interface RetentionCandidate { readonly runId: string; readonly workspaceId: string; readonly status: RunStatus; readonly projectedToRepo: boolean; readonly createdAtMs: number }` (reuse the existing `RunStatus` type from the store types — import the TYPE only, which is erased at compile time; do not import store runtime code).
   - `interface RetentionConfig { readonly keepLastNPerWorkspace: number }`
   - `const DEFAULT_KEEP_LAST_N = 25`
   - `function resolveRetentionConfig(raw: unknown): RetentionConfig` — coerces a parsed settings blob into a config: reads `keepLastNPerWorkspace` (or a clearly-named equivalent you document) when it is a positive integer, otherwise falls back to DEFAULT_KEEP_LAST_N. Reject/ignore non-positive, non-integer, NaN, and missing values by falling back to the default. Do NOT read any file here — caller passes the already-parsed object; this keeps the function pure and testable.
   - `function selectRunsToPrune(candidates: readonly RetentionCandidate[], config: RetentionConfig): { prune: string[]; keep: string[] }`

2. SELECTION RULES (these are the safety invariants — encode them exactly):
   - Group candidates by `workspaceId`. Fairness is mandatory: one workspace's run count must NEVER influence another's keep/prune decision. Each workspace is evaluated entirely independently.
   - Within a workspace, order by recency using `createdAtMs` descending (newest first); break ties by `runId` descending for determinism.
   - A run is prune-eligible ONLY IF ALL hold: (a) its status is TERMINAL — one of `completed`, `failed`, `stopped`; AND (b) `projectedToRepo === true` (its durable record is confirmed written to the repo's cocoder/runs/); AND (c) it sits beyond rank N within its workspace (i.e. it is not among the newest N).
   - Any run that is NON-TERMINAL (`running`, `awaiting-founder`, `awaiting-archive-confirmation`, `held`) is ALWAYS kept, regardless of rank or age. Any run with `projectedToRepo === false` is ALWAYS kept, regardless of rank. These force-kept runs still occupy their recency rank position (they are not removed from the ordering used to compute 'newest N').
   - `prune` = the runIds selected for pruning; `keep` = all other input runIds. Every input runId appears in exactly one of the two arrays; arrays are deterministically ordered.

3. TESTS — new `packages/core/tests/retention.test.ts`, fixture-only, no real paths, no I/O. Pin at minimum:
   - keep-last-N-per-workspace: with N=2 and workspace A having 5 terminal+projected runs, the 2 newest are kept and the 3 oldest pruned.
   - fairness: workspace A with 50 runs and workspace B with 3 runs — B's 3 runs are ALL kept; A's churn does not evict any B run. Each workspace bounded independently to N.
   - non-terminal exclusion: a `running` / `awaiting-founder` / `awaiting-archive-confirmation` / `held` run beyond rank N is NOT pruned (cover each status).
   - projection-gating: a terminal run with `projectedToRepo=false` beyond rank N is NOT pruned.
   - idempotency: feeding only the `keep` set back into selectRunsToPrune returns an empty `prune`.
   - config: resolveRetentionConfig returns 25 by default and for invalid inputs (0, -1, 1.5, NaN, missing, wrong type); honors a valid positive integer override.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- `pnpm -C packages/core typecheck` (or the repo's typecheck) and the new test file pass; existing tests still pass.
- The module imports NO runtime I/O (verify: it does not import node:fs / node:path / the store implementation; a type-only import of RunStatus is fine).
- Export the new symbols from the appropriate barrel ONLY if existing sibling modules (e.g. run-dir.ts) are re-exported there; match the existing export convention — do not invent a new export surface.
- Do not wire this into the daemon, runner boot, or any deletion path yet — that is a later atom. Leave the live store and local/runs untouched.

Return: the diff, the exact test command you ran, and its passing output.

data.reason: Pure retention.ts (type-only RunStatus import, zero I/O) encodes all four invariants — per-workspace fairness, terminal+rank gating, non-terminal exclusion, projection-gating, idempotency, config default 25; 19 fixture tests pass and core typecheck is clean.

data.task:
ATOM 1 of the machine-local cache retention priority — build the NON-DESTRUCTIVE retention PLAN layer: materialize RetentionCandidate[] from the live store + projection check, compute the prune plan via atom 0's selectRunsToPrune, and LOG it. STILL ZERO deletion: NO removal of local/runs dirs, NO SQLite row trim, NO WAL checkpoint, NO log rotation. This atom only answers 'what WOULD be pruned' and surfaces it. The actual destructive trim is a later, separately-verified atom.

CONTEXT / primitives already in the tree (use them, do not reinvent):
- `selectRunsToPrune`, `RetentionCandidate`, `RetentionConfig`, `DEFAULT_KEEP_LAST_N`, `resolveRetentionConfig` from `packages/core/src/runner/retention.ts` (atom 0, exported via runner/index.ts).
- Store read: `store.listRuns()` (no filter) returns ALL runs across workspaces, newest-first, NO implicit cap. Each `Run` has `{ id, workspaceId, status, createdAt /* epoch ms */, endedAt }` (see packages/core/src/store/types.ts).
- Projection confirmation primitive: `readPortableRunById(primaryRoot, runId)` in `packages/core/src/store/portable/runs.ts` returns the portable run record (`PortableRunFile`) from that repo's `cocoder/runs/<n>-<runId>/run.json`, or `null` if absent. NON-NULL ⇒ the durable record is confirmed projected to the repo (this is the projectedToRepo gate). It is async.
- A run's repo path is the workspace's `Workspace.path` (absolute repo root). NOTE: the store has NO listWorkspaces method — so DO NOT hard-wire a workspace lookup inside the core function; take it as an injected dependency (see below). Finding the real runtime workspace→path resolver is the NEXT atom's concern, not this one.

WHAT TO BUILD — new module `packages/core/src/runner/retention-plan.ts`:
  - `interface RetentionPlan { readonly config: RetentionConfig; readonly candidates: readonly RetentionCandidate[]; readonly prune: readonly string[]; readonly keep: readonly string[]; readonly perWorkspace: ReadonlyArray<{ workspaceId: string; total: number; kept: number; pruned: number }> }`
  - `interface RetentionPlanDeps { listAllRuns(): readonly Run[]; isProjectedToRepo(run: Run): Promise<boolean> | boolean }` — fully injected so the core is testable with an in-memory store + a fake projection fn, touching NO real install path.
  - `async function planRetention(deps: RetentionPlanDeps, config: RetentionConfig): Promise<RetentionPlan>`:
      1. `const runs = deps.listAllRuns()`.
      2. For each run, resolve `projectedToRepo = await deps.isProjectedToRepo(run)` and build a `RetentionCandidate { runId: run.id, workspaceId: run.workspaceId, status: run.status, projectedToRepo, createdAtMs: run.createdAt }`.
      3. `const { prune, keep } = selectRunsToPrune(candidates, config)`.
      4. Compute `perWorkspace` rollup (total/kept/pruned counts per workspaceId, deterministically ordered by workspaceId).
      5. Return the full RetentionPlan. PURE of side effects except reading via deps — NO deletion.
  - `function formatRetentionPlan(plan: RetentionPlan): string` — a concise, human-readable multiline summary: the config N, and per workspace `kept/total (pruned M)`, plus the list of prune runIds. This is the surface-the-policy requirement (no silent deletion); a caller logs this string. Keep it deterministic.
  - `function projectionCheckerFor(resolveRepoPath: (workspaceId: string) => string | null): (run: Run) => Promise<boolean>` — a thin DEFAULT adapter that, given a workspaceId→repo-path resolver, returns an `isProjectedToRepo` that calls `readPortableRunById(repoPath, run.id)` and returns `repoPath != null && (await readPortableRunById(repoPath, run.id)) !== null`. A run whose repo path cannot be resolved is treated as NOT projected (force-kept) — fail SAFE, never prune when projection cannot be confirmed. Import readPortableRunById from the portable module. (This adapter is the only part touching fs, and only via the existing read-only portable reader — still no deletion.)
  - Export the new symbols via runner/index.ts following the exact convention used for retention.ts / run-dir.ts.

TESTS — new `packages/core/tests/retention-plan.test.ts`, FIXTURE-ONLY (execution-safety hard rule: a test must NEVER touch real install paths). Use `openRunStore(':memory:')` (see how existing tests in packages/core/tests/store.test.ts open it) to create runs across two workspaces with controlled createdAt/status, and a FAKE `isProjectedToRepo`. Pin:
  - planRetention with an in-memory store of N+extra terminal+projected runs in workspace A and a few in workspace B produces the expected prune/keep split and correct perWorkspace rollup; B is unaffected by A's churn.
  - fail-safe projection: a run for which the injected projection fn returns false is force-kept even beyond rank N.
  - non-terminal force-keep flows through (a held/awaiting run beyond rank N is kept) — exercise via the real selectRunsToPrune path, not a re-implementation.
  - formatRetentionPlan output contains the config N and each workspace's counts and is deterministic (assert exact string or stable substrings).
  - projectionCheckerFor: with a fake resolveRepoPath returning null for an unknown workspace, the checker returns false (force-keep) and does NOT throw. (You may unit-test the resolver-null branch without hitting fs; do not call readPortableRunById against a real path.)

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Repo typecheck and the two new test files pass; existing tests still pass (run the core suite, not just the new file, to confirm no regression).
- ZERO destructive behavior introduced: grep your own diff to confirm no fs unlink/rm/rmdir, no SQL DELETE, no WAL/PRAGMA, no log rotation. The only fs touch is the existing read-only `readPortableRunById`.
- No daemon/runner-boot wiring yet; nothing calls planRetention in production code paths in this atom. Leave the live store and local/runs untouched.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands you ran, and their passing output.

data.reason: retention-plan.ts is injection-based and non-destructive (grep confirms no unlink/DELETE/PRAGMA/rotate; only the read-only readPortableRunById); projectionCheckerFor fails safe to false; 5 fixture tests use :memory: store + fake projection, core typecheck clean, full core suite 685/685 with no regression.

data.task:
ATOM 2 of the machine-local cache retention priority — implement the STORE-ROW TRIM primitive with fault-recurrence preservation. This is the first destructive mechanism, so it is fixture-only and NOT yet wired to the daemon (a later atom does flag-gated wiring). It must only ever affect the exact runIds it is told to prune. Folder GC and WAL/log housekeeping are SEPARATE later atoms — do NOT touch local/runs dirs, the WAL, or logs here.

FIRST, READ for exact names (do not guess): `packages/core/src/store/schema.ts` (table + column names, and whether any FK has ON DELETE CASCADE), and `packages/core/src/store/sqlite-store.ts` around `listFaultHistory` (line ~305) and `listRuns`. The fault-recurrence read is `listFaultHistory(workspaceId)` which joins the `event` table (rows with `type='fault-triaged'`) to the `run` table on `workspace_id`. So recurrence needs BOTH the run row AND that run's `fault-triaged` event rows to survive.

ADD a focused deletion primitive on the store — a new RunStore interface method (add to `packages/core/src/store/types.ts` RunStore interface AND implement in `sqlite-store.ts`). Suggested signature: `pruneRunRows(runId: string): { runRowKept: boolean; faultEventsKept: number }`. Semantics, executed inside a SINGLE transaction for atomicity:
  1. Determine whether this run has >=1 `fault-triaged` event.
  2. IF it has fault events: DELETE this run's rows from session, work_item, commit_link, AND its NON-fault events (`event WHERE run_id=? AND type != 'fault-triaged'`). KEEP the `run` row and its `fault-triaged` event rows. Return `{ runRowKept: true, faultEventsKept: <count> }`.
  3. IF it has NO fault events: DELETE all of this run's rows from session, work_item, commit_link, event, AND the `run` row itself. Return `{ runRowKept: false, faultEventsKept: 0 }`.
  - Use the real column/table names from schema.ts. If event.run_id (etc.) already has ON DELETE CASCADE from run, you may leverage it for the no-fault path, but the fault-bearing path MUST selectively keep the run row + fault events regardless of cascade — verify the cascade direction before relying on it. One WHERE per statement (ADR-0003 style). Pruning a runId that does not exist is a harmless no-op (return runRowKept:false, faultEventsKept:0), never an error.
  - This method deletes ONLY rows for the single passed runId. It must NOT iterate, must NOT consult retention policy, must NOT decide what to prune — selection already happened in atom 0/1. Keep it a dumb, well-scoped primitive.

TESTS — new `packages/core/tests/retention-trim.test.ts`, FIXTURE-ONLY via `openRunStore(':memory:')` (never real install paths). Build runs with sessions/work-items/commit-links/events using the store's create/record methods. Pin:
  - no-fault prune: a pruned run with sessions+events+commits but NO fault-triaged event is fully removed — getRun(runId) returns null afterward, and listSessions/listWorkItems/listCommitLinks/listEvents are empty. runRowKept === false.
  - fault-bearing prune: a pruned run that has a `fault-triaged` event (record one via recordEvent) — after pruneRunRows, its run row STILL exists (getRun != null), its `fault-triaged` event(s) remain, its NON-fault events/sessions/work-items/commit-links are gone, and CRUCIALLY `store.listFaultHistory(workspaceId)` STILL returns that run's fault (recurrence survives the trim). runRowKept === true, faultEventsKept matches.
  - isolation: pruning run X never deletes rows belonging to a sibling run Y in the same workspace (Y's sessions/events/run row all intact).
  - idempotency: calling pruneRunRows twice on the same runId is safe and the second call is a no-op-shaped result.
  - unknown runId: pruneRunRows('nope') does not throw and returns {runRowKept:false, faultEventsKept:0}.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Repo typecheck passes and the FULL core suite still passes (run `pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run`, not just the new file) — listFaultHistory and existing store behavior must be unchanged for non-pruned runs.
- The deletion is transactional (a failure mid-trim does not leave a half-deleted run).
- NO folder deletion, NO WAL checkpoint/PRAGMA, NO log rotation, NO daemon/runner wiring in this atom. The primitive is callable but nothing in production calls it yet.
- Confirm in your write-up: which path (cascade vs explicit deletes) you used and why recurrence is preserved.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands you ran, and their passing output (including the full-suite pass count).

data.reason: pruneRunRows is transactional and single-run-scoped: no-fault runs deleted whole, fault-bearing runs keep run row + fault-triaged events so listFaultHistory still returns the fault post-trim (recurrence preserved); sibling isolation, idempotency, unknown-id no-op all pinned; new inTransaction helper is net-new (no duplicate owner); typecheck clean, full core suite 690/690.

data.task:
ATOM 3 of the machine-local cache retention priority — implement the FOLDER GC primitive that removes a single pruned run's machine-local scratch dir. This is destructive on the filesystem but FIXTURE-ONLY (temp dirs); it is NOT wired to the daemon (a later atom does flag-gated wiring) and must touch NOTHING outside the run dir it is told to remove. Store trim and WAL/log housekeeping are SEPARATE atoms — do NOT touch the SQLite store or logs here.

LOCATION: add to `packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts` — it is the SOLE OWNER of the machine-local run-dir layout (`localRunDir`, `resolveLocalRunDir`, `migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs`). The new GC function lives here and REUSES the existing `resolveLocalRunDir(runsRoot, runId)` resolver (it already prefers the nested `runsRoot/<workspaceId>/<runId>` layout and falls back to legacy flat `runsRoot/<runId>`). Do NOT duplicate path-resolution logic — call resolveLocalRunDir.

ADD: `export function removeLocalRunDir(runsRoot: string, runId: string): { removed: string | null }` with this behavior:
  1. `const dir = resolveLocalRunDir(runsRoot, runId)`. If it returns null (no nested and no legacy dir exists), return `{ removed: null }` — a clean idempotent no-op, never an error.
  2. SAFETY GUARD before deleting: confirm the resolved `dir` is strictly INSIDE `runsRoot` (e.g. the resolved absolute path starts with the absolute runsRoot + path separator and is not equal to runsRoot itself). If it is not strictly inside runsRoot, do NOT delete — throw a clear Error. This prevents a malformed runId (e.g. containing '..' or an absolute path) from escaping the runs root. (resolveLocalRunDir only ever returns join(runsRoot, ...) paths, but add the guard as defense-in-depth since this function deletes recursively.)
  3. Recursively remove `dir` (fs `rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true })`).
  4. After removal, if the run lived in a NESTED layout (its parent dir is `runsRoot/<workspaceId>`, i.e. parent !== runsRoot), and that parent workspace dir is now EMPTY, remove the empty parent dir too (best-effort: swallow errors; never remove a non-empty dir; never remove runsRoot itself). This keeps orphan empty workspace dirs from accumulating. A legacy-flat removal (parent === runsRoot) skips this step.
  5. Return `{ removed: dir }`.
  - Idempotent: calling twice is safe (second call resolves null → no-op). Tolerant of a concurrently-deleted dir (force:true). Deletes ONLY the one run's dir (plus a now-empty workspace parent); never iterates other runs, never consults retention policy — selection happened upstream.
  - Export the new symbol via runner/index.ts following the existing run-dir.ts export convention.

TESTS — new `packages/core/tests/retention-folder-gc.test.ts`, FIXTURE-ONLY using a temp dir created via node:os `tmpdir()` + `mkdtempSync` (or fs.mkdtemp). NEVER reference real install paths (no local/runs of this checkout). Clean up the temp root in afterEach. Build a fake runsRoot with nested + legacy dirs (write a marker file inside each) and pin:
  - nested removal: `runsRoot/ws-a/run_1` (with a file inside) is removed; removeLocalRunDir returns its path; the dir no longer exists.
  - empty-parent cleanup: after removing the only run in `ws-a`, the now-empty `runsRoot/ws-a` dir is also gone; but if `ws-a` still has a sibling run dir, `ws-a` is RETAINED and the sibling untouched.
  - legacy flat removal: a flat `runsRoot/run_legacy` dir is removed and runsRoot itself is NOT removed.
  - sibling isolation: removing `run_1` never deletes `run_2` in the same or another workspace.
  - missing run: removeLocalRunDir(runsRoot, 'run_absent') returns { removed: null } and does not throw.
  - guard: a runId that would resolve outside runsRoot is rejected (you can assert the guard by constructing a case where the path escapes, OR assert resolveLocalRunDir-based paths always stay inside; at minimum prove runsRoot itself is never deleted).
  - idempotency: calling removeLocalRunDir twice on the same runId is safe (second returns { removed: null }).

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Repo typecheck passes and the FULL core suite still passes (`pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run`).
- No SQLite/store access, no WAL/PRAGMA, no log rotation, no daemon/runner wiring in this atom.
- The only filesystem mutation is removing the targeted run dir (+ its now-empty nested workspace parent). Confirm via reading your own diff that no other path is deleted and that the inside-runsRoot guard is present.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands you ran, and their passing output (including full-suite pass count).

data.reason: removeLocalRunDir reuses resolveLocalRunDir, deletes only the targeted nested/legacy run dir plus a now-empty workspace parent, and is gated by a resolved-path strict-inside-runsRoot guard that a ../escaped test proves rejects traversal while leaving the outside dir intact; sibling isolation, empty-parent cleanup, missing-run no-op, idempotency all pinned with temp-dir-only fixtures; typecheck clean, full core suite 698/698.

data.task:
ATOM 4 of the machine-local cache retention priority — implement the two HOUSEKEEPING primitives: (A) a WAL checkpoint method on the store, and (B) a generic size-based log-rotation utility. Both are fixture-only and NOT wired into the daemon/cadence yet (atom 5 supplies config + call sites). Keep them small, parameterized primitives — no policy decisions, no daemon access.

PART A — WAL checkpoint on the store:
- The store opens in WAL mode (`openRunStore` runs `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`). Add a RunStore interface method `checkpointWal(): { busy: number; log: number; checkpointed: number }` (add to `packages/core/src/store/types.ts` RunStore interface AND implement in `packages/core/src/store/sqlite-store.ts`).
- Implementation: run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` and return its single result row mapped to `{ busy, log, checkpointed }` (node:sqlite returns a row with columns `busy`, `log`, `checkpointed`; coerce to numbers, default 0 if absent). TRUNCATE mode checkpoints the WAL and truncates the WAL file to reclaim space. Must not throw on an idle db; `busy>0` is a valid (not-error) outcome meaning a reader held it — just return it. Do NOT wrap in a write transaction (a checkpoint is not a transaction).
- TEST in a NEW `packages/core/tests/retention-wal.test.ts`, FIXTURE-ONLY but using a TEMP-FILE db (NOT ':memory:', because WAL is a no-op for in-memory dbs) created under `mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(),...))`; close the store and remove the temp dir in afterEach. Pin: open a file-backed store, write several runs/events to grow the WAL, call `checkpointWal()`, assert it returns a result object (numbers) without throwing AND that previously-written data is still readable afterward (e.g. listRuns still returns the rows). Never touch the real install db.

PART B — generic log rotation utility:
- New module `packages/core/src/runner/log-rotation.ts` exporting `function rotateLogFile(path: string, opts: { maxBytes: number; keep: number }): { rotated: boolean; sizeBytes: number }`.
- Semantics: if `path` does not exist, return `{ rotated: false, sizeBytes: 0 }` (no-op). Stat the file; if its size is `< maxBytes`, return `{ rotated: false, sizeBytes }` unchanged. If size `>= maxBytes`, ROTATE: drop the oldest by deleting `path.<keep>` if present, then shift `path.<keep-1>` -> `path.<keep>`, ..., `path.1` -> `path.2`, then `path` -> `path.1`. After rotation the active `path` no longer exists (the next writer recreates it by appending). Return `{ rotated: true, sizeBytes }` (the size that triggered rotation). `keep` must be >= 1 (clamp or validate); `keep` rotated generations are retained, older are discarded. Use synchronous fs (existsSync/statSync/renameSync/rmSync) for determinism; tolerate a missing intermediate generation (a gap is fine — only rename generations that exist).
- This utility is GENERIC (operates on any path); it does NOT know about oz-audit.log or turn logs — atom 5 points it at `local/oz-audit.log` and the `local/oz/` turn logs.
- TEST in a NEW `packages/core/tests/retention-log-rotation.test.ts`, FIXTURE-ONLY with a temp dir (mkdtempSync), cleaned up in afterEach. Pin: (1) below-threshold file is untouched (rotated:false); (2) at/over-threshold file rotates to `.1` and the active path is gone; (3) repeated rotations shift `.1`->`.2` and cap at `keep` (the `.<keep+1>` generation never exists; oldest is discarded); (4) missing file is a no-op; (5) a gap in generations (e.g. `.1` exists but `.2` missing) rotates without throwing.
- Export rotateLogFile via runner/index.ts following the existing convention.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Repo typecheck passes and the FULL core suite still passes (`pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run`).
- checkpointWal touches ONLY the WAL/db it is called on; rotateLogFile mutates ONLY the given path and its numbered siblings within the same directory. No daemon/runner wiring, no retention-policy logic, no real install paths in tests.
- Confirm in your write-up that the WAL test uses a temp file db (not :memory:) and that rotation never deletes anything other than the over-`keep` oldest generation.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands you ran, and their passing output (including full-suite pass count).

data.reason: checkpointWal runs PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) (no transaction) returning numeric busy/log/checkpointed, tested on a temp-file db with data still readable after; rotateLogFile no-ops on missing/under-threshold, rotates at >=maxBytes shifting .1->.N, caps at keep, tolerates generation gaps; both fixture-only, exports wired, typecheck clean, full core suite 704/704.

data.task:
ATOM 5 of the machine-local cache retention priority — implement the GC ORCHESTRATOR `runRetentionGc` that composes the four already-built primitives into one pass, with the flag-gate, the never-prune-the-running-run guard, live-store-mutation tolerance, and full logging. It composes over INJECTED dependencies so it is fully fixture-testable with NO real install path. This atom does NOT wire into the daemon and does NOT read any config file — that is the NEXT atom (atom 6). Ship-inert is enforced here via the `enabled` flag defaulting OFF.

PRIMITIVES TO COMPOSE (all already in the tree, exported from runner/index.ts or store/index.ts):
- `planRetention(deps, config)` + `formatRetentionPlan(plan)` (retention-plan.ts) — builds the per-workspace keep/prune plan and a human-readable summary.
- `RetentionConfig` / `resolveRetentionConfig` / `DEFAULT_KEEP_LAST_N` (retention.ts).
- store `pruneRunRows(runId): PruneRunRowsResult` (recurrence-preserving store-row trim).
- store `checkpointWal(): CheckpointWalResult`.
- `removeLocalRunDir(runsRoot, runId)` (run-dir.ts) and `rotateLogFile(path, opts)` (log-rotation.ts) — but the orchestrator takes these as INJECTED thunks (see deps), so atom 6 can bind the real runsRoot/log paths.

FIRST extend the config (retention.ts) to carry the ship-inert flag:
- Add `readonly enabled: boolean` to `RetentionConfig`. Update `resolveRetentionConfig(raw)` to read `enabled` as a boolean, DEFAULTING TO `false` (OFF) when missing or non-boolean. Update `DEFAULT` construction and atom 0's tests that assert the default config object so they still pass (default is now `{ enabled: false, keepLastNPerWorkspace: 25 }`). Keep keepLastNPerWorkspace coercion exactly as-is.

NEW module `packages/core/src/runner/retention-gc.ts`:
  - `interface RetentionGcDeps {
      listAllRuns(): readonly Run[]
      isProjectedToRepo(run: Run): Promise<boolean> | boolean
      pruneRunRows(runId: string): PruneRunRowsResult
      removeRunDir(runId: string): { removed: string | null }
      checkpointWal(): CheckpointWalResult
      rotateLogs(): void
      currentRunId: string | null   // the run currently executing — NEVER pruned, even if selection somehow lists it
      log(message: string): void
    }`
  - `interface RetentionGcResult {
      enabled: boolean
      prunedRunIds: string[]
      skippedCurrentRun: boolean
      storeRunRowsKept: number    // count of pruned runs whose run row was kept for recurrence
      dirsRemoved: number
      failures: Array<{ runId: string; stage: 'store' | 'dir'; message: string }>
      wal: CheckpointWalResult | null
    }`
  - `async function runRetentionGc(deps: RetentionGcDeps, config: RetentionConfig): Promise<RetentionGcResult>`:
    1. IF `config.enabled !== true`: `deps.log('retention GC disabled (enabled=false); no-op')` and RETURN `{ enabled: false, prunedRunIds: [], skippedCurrentRun: false, storeRunRowsKept: 0, dirsRemoved: 0, failures: [], wal: null }`. Make ZERO destructive calls (no prune, no removeRunDir, no checkpoint, no rotate). This is the ship-inert guarantee — pin it in a test.
    2. Build the plan: `const plan = await planRetention({ listAllRuns: deps.listAllRuns, isProjectedToRepo: deps.isProjectedToRepo }, config)`. `deps.log(formatRetentionPlan(plan))` — surface the plan (no silent deletion).
    3. Compute the final prune list: start from `plan.prune`, then EXPLICITLY drop `deps.currentRunId` if present (belt-and-suspenders beyond selection's non-terminal exclusion). Set `skippedCurrentRun = true` iff currentRunId was in plan.prune.
    4. For each runId to prune, in deterministic order: FIRST `deps.removeRunDir(runId)` (folder), THEN `deps.pruneRunRows(runId)` (store row). RATIONALE for folder-before-store: if the pass dies mid-run, the surviving store row drives a retry next pass; deleting the store row first would orphan the folder. Wrap EACH primitive call in its own try/catch — a throw from one stage records a `failures` entry ({runId, stage, message}) and CONTINUES the pass (live-store-mutation tolerance: a run vanishing underneath must not abort the whole GC). Tally dirsRemoved (removed !== null) and storeRunRowsKept (result.runRowKept).
    5. AFTER the loop (always, when enabled): `const wal = deps.checkpointWal()` and `deps.rotateLogs()`, each wrapped so a housekeeping failure is logged but does not throw. Log a one-line summary (counts).
    6. Return the populated RetentionGcResult.
  - Export runRetentionGc + its types via runner/index.ts (existing convention).
  - PURE of ambient I/O: it only acts through injected deps. Do NOT import node:fs / the store directly in this module (the thunks are injected). Importing TYPES (Run, PruneRunRowsResult, CheckpointWalResult, RetentionConfig) is fine.

TESTS — new `packages/core/tests/retention-gc.test.ts`, FIXTURE-ONLY. Use a `:memory:` store for realistic runs AND/OR hand-built fake deps with spy counters; never a real install path. Pin AT MINIMUM:
  - DISABLED no-op: with `enabled:false`, runRetentionGc makes zero pruneRunRows/removeRunDir/checkpointWal/rotateLogs calls and returns enabled:false. (Use spy thunks asserting call counts === 0.)
  - happy path: enabled, N=2, workspace with 5 terminal+projected runs → the 3 oldest are pruned: removeRunDir called for each (folder), pruneRunRows called for each (store), checkpointWal called once, rotateLogs called once; result counts match.
  - ORDER: assert removeRunDir is invoked BEFORE pruneRunRows for a given run (e.g. via a shared call-order log).
  - never-prune-running: set `currentRunId` to a run that would otherwise be in the prune set (construct it terminal+projected+beyond-N), assert it is NOT pruned (no removeRunDir/pruneRunRows for it) and skippedCurrentRun===true.
  - projection-gating end-to-end: an unprojected run beyond N is never pruned.
  - recurrence end-to-end: a fault-bearing pruned run keeps its run row + fault history — assert `listFaultHistory` (via the :memory: store) still returns it after the pass; storeRunRowsKept reflects it.
  - live-mutation tolerance: a deps.pruneRunRows (or removeRunDir) that THROWS for ONE run records a failures entry and the pass still processes the others and still runs checkpointWal/rotateLogs.
  - config: resolveRetentionConfig default now includes enabled:false; a valid `{enabled:true, keepLastNPerWorkspace:n}` is honored; non-boolean enabled falls back to false.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Repo typecheck passes and the FULL core suite still passes (`pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run`) — INCLUDING atom 0's retention.test.ts which you must update for the new `enabled` default (do not weaken its other assertions).
- No daemon/runner wiring, no config-file reading, no real install paths in this atom. Nothing in production calls runRetentionGc yet.
- Reading your own diff, confirm: disabled path makes zero destructive calls; folder-before-store ordering; every destructive call is individually try/caught.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands you ran, and their passing output (including full-suite pass count).

data.reason: runRetentionGc composes the four primitives deps-only: disabled flag makes zero destructive/housekeeping calls; folder-before-store ordering, per-call try/catch with failures-continue, currentRunId explicitly skipped, projection-gating and fault-recurrence survival all pinned adversarially; config gains enabled (default off) with atom0/1 tests updated; typecheck clean, full core suite 713/713.

data.task:
ATOM 6 of the machine-local cache retention priority — two small, still-INERT prep changes ahead of the live daemon wiring (atom 7): (A) generalize the GC's never-prune guard from a single current run to a SET of protected runs (the multi-workspace daemon has many actively-driven runs at once), and (B) surface the retention config in the daemon's existing settings module. NO daemon boot/periodic call site yet — nothing in production calls runRetentionGc after this atom. Fixture-only tests.

PART A — widen the guard in `packages/core/src/runner/retention-gc.ts`:
- In `RetentionGcDeps`, REPLACE `readonly currentRunId: string | null` with `readonly protectedRunIds: ReadonlySet<string>` — the set of runs that must NEVER be pruned regardless of selection (e.g. runs this daemon is actively driving, including a terminal-but-still-finalizing run). An empty set means 'rely on status-based exclusion only'.
- In `RetentionGcResult`, REPLACE `skippedCurrentRun: boolean` with `readonly skippedProtectedRunIds: string[]` — the subset of `plan.prune` that was withheld because it was in protectedRunIds (deterministically ordered as they appear in plan.prune). For the disabled no-op return it as `[]`.
- Update the body: `const skippedProtectedRunIds = plan.prune.filter((id) => deps.protectedRunIds.has(id))`; `const runIdsToPrune = plan.prune.filter((id) => !deps.protectedRunIds.has(id))`. Everything else (folder-before-store ordering, per-call try/catch, housekeeping-always-runs) stays exactly as committed.
- Update `packages/core/tests/retention-gc.test.ts`: the `gcDeps` default now uses `protectedRunIds: new Set()`; the 'never prunes the current run' test passes `protectedRunIds: new Set([<runId>])` and asserts `result.skippedProtectedRunIds` contains it and it is neither dir-removed nor store-trimmed. Keep ALL other assertions (disabled no-op, order, projection-gating, recurrence, failure-continues) intact.

PART B — retention settings in `packages/daemon/src/settings.ts`:
- The `Settings` interface + `local/settings.json` is the daemon's settings home (it already has pollIntervalMs, defaultWorkspaceId, ozAutoCompactRuns). Add `readonly retention: RetentionConfig` to `Settings`, where `RetentionConfig` is the EXACT type from `@cocoder/core` (import it — do NOT redefine the shape). RetentionConfig is `{ enabled: boolean; keepLastNPerWorkspace: number }` and its coercion owner is core's `resolveRetentionConfig(raw)` (default `{ enabled: false, keepLastNPerWorkspace: 25 }`).
- In `DEFAULT_SETTINGS`, set `retention: resolveRetentionConfig(undefined)` (i.e. the core default — enabled:false, keepLastNPerWorkspace:25). Do NOT hand-write the literal; derive it from the core owner so there is one source of truth.
- In `saneSettings`, set `retention: resolveRetentionConfig(record.retention)` — delegate ALL retention validation to core's resolver (single owner; do not re-validate keepLastNPerWorkspace or enabled inside settings.ts).
- In `sanePatch`, if the patch body has an own `retention` property, include `patch.retention = resolveRetentionConfig(record.retention)` so a settings write can toggle it; otherwise leave it absent (so an unrelated patch does not clobber retention). Match the existing hasOwnProperty pattern used for ozAutoCompactRuns.
- Add/extend the daemon settings test (find the existing settings test under packages/daemon/tests/, or add `packages/daemon/tests/settings-retention.test.ts` if none covers settings) to pin: default settings carry `retention:{enabled:false,keepLastNPerWorkspace:25}`; reading a settings.json with `retention:{enabled:true,keepLastNPerWorkspace:10}` yields that; an invalid retention blob falls back to the core default; a patch without `retention` preserves the existing retention value while a patch with `retention` updates it. FIXTURE-ONLY — use a temp cocoderHome (mkdtemp), never the real install settings.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Repo typecheck passes for BOTH packages (`pnpm -C packages/core typecheck` and `pnpm -C packages/daemon typecheck`) and BOTH test suites pass (`pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run` and `pnpm -C packages/daemon exec vitest run`). The widened guard must not regress any retention-gc test; the settings change must not regress existing settings/daemon tests (existing settings.json files without a retention key must still load with the default, since saneSettings tolerates a missing key).
- Still INERT: no call site invokes runRetentionGc; the daemon does not yet schedule or run a GC pass. This atom only changes a type/guard and the settings schema.
- Single-owner check: retention coercion lives ONLY in core's resolveRetentionConfig; settings.ts and the daemon import it, never re-implement it.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands you ran for both packages, and their passing output (with pass counts).

data.task:
ATOM 7 (final wiring) of the machine-local cache retention priority — add the DAEMON BOOT call site that binds the real install paths and runs ONE retention GC pass at startup, gated by settings so it SHIPS INERT (enabled defaults false → the pass no-ops, mutating nothing). Boot-only trigger (no periodic timer this atom — a periodic cadence is a deliberate follow-up). This is the only atom that executes against the live install, so correctness of the gate and bindings is paramount.

WHERE: add `export async function runRetentionGcOnce(ctx: OzContext): Promise<void>` to `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` (mirror the existing boot helpers `reconcileOrphans` / `migrateLegacyRunDirsOnce` in shape and error-tolerance), and CALL it from `packages/daemon/src/server.ts` at boot AFTER `migrateLegacyRunDirsOnce` (around line 235, after reconcileOrphans on line 234). Boot ordering matters: reconcileOrphans (marks stranded 'running' rows failed) and the legacy-dir migration must complete BEFORE GC so GC sees correct statuses and the nested layout.

BINDINGS for `runRetentionGcOnce(ctx)`:
1. `const settings = await readSettings(ctx.cocoderHome)` (from ./settings.js); `const config = settings.retention` (the RetentionConfig added in atom 6). If `config.enabled !== true`, you may early-return WITHOUT building deps (the orchestrator also no-ops, but skipping the work entirely is the cleanest inert guarantee) — though still call runRetentionGc so the disabled-log line is emitted is acceptable too; pick the simpler and pin whichever you choose. Either way: ZERO mutation when disabled.
2. Build a workspaceId→repoPath map: collect the DISTINCT `workspaceId`s from `ctx.store.listRuns()`, resolve each via `findWorkspace(ctx.cocoderHome, workspaceId)` (from ./registry.js, async; returns an entry with `.path` or null/undefined), into a `Map<string,string>`. Unresolved workspaces map to nothing (projection will treat them as not-projected → force-kept, fail-safe).
3. `isProjectedToRepo`: use core's `projectionCheckerFor((workspaceId) => map.get(workspaceId) ?? null)` — this confirms each run's durable record exists in its repo's cocoder/runs/ before the run is eligible (the projection-gating safety invariant).
4. `protectedRunIds`: a `Set<string>` = the union of (a) `ctx.inFlight` values that are real runIds (exclude the literal 'pending'), and (b) `ctx.stopControllers` keys — i.e. every run this daemon process is actively driving. (At boot these are empty, but bind them properly for correctness and the future periodic trigger.)
5. `removeRunDir: (runId) => removeLocalRunDir(ctx.runsRoot, runId)` (core).
6. `rotateLogs`: rotate `ozAuditPath(ctx.cocoderHome)` (from ./audit.js → local/oz-audit.log) AND each regular file in the `local/oz/` turn-log dir (join(ctx.cocoderHome,'local','oz')), each via core's `rotateLogFile(path, { maxBytes, keep })`. Use sane constants: `maxBytes = 8 * 1024 * 1024` (8MB), `keep = 3`. Tolerate a missing oz dir (no-op).
7. `log`: emit a line via the daemon's existing logging convention (console.error or the same channel other boot helpers use) AND append one summary audit entry `appendAudit(ctx.cocoderHome, { action: 'retention-gc', ...counts })` after the pass.
8. Call `const result = await runRetentionGc(deps, config)`; wrap the WHOLE function body in try/catch so a GC failure logs but NEVER blocks or fails daemon boot (boot resilience — same posture as reconcileOrphans).

IMPORTS: `runRetentionGc`, `projectionCheckerFor`, `removeLocalRunDir`, `rotateLogFile` from `@cocoder/core`; `readSettings` from ./settings.js; `findWorkspace` from ./registry.js; `ozAuditPath`, `appendAudit` from ./audit.js. Reuse — do not re-implement any of these.

TESTS — extend the daemon suite using the EXISTING OzContext test fixture/harness that other boot-helper tests use (look at how `reconcileOrphans` is tested under packages/daemon/tests/, e.g. mutations.test.ts or a launcher test). FIXTURE-ONLY: temp cocoderHome (mkdtemp), in-memory or temp-file store, fake run dirs under a temp runsRoot — NEVER the real install. Pin AT MINIMUM:
  - INERT-WHEN-DISABLED (non-negotiable): with default settings (no settings.json, or retention.enabled=false), runRetentionGcOnce(ctx) performs ZERO mutation — every run row in the store still present after, and every seeded run-dir still exists on disk. This is the live-install safety guarantee; assert it explicitly.
  - ENABLED happy path: write settings.json with `retention:{enabled:true,keepLastNPerWorkspace:<small>}`; register a workspace (so findWorkspace resolves its repo path) and project the durable records for the runs that should be eligible (write the portable cocoder/runs/<n>-<runId>/run.json so projectionCheckerFor sees them — reuse the portable writer if a helper exists); seed >N projected terminal runs + their run-dirs; run; assert the beyond-N projected terminal runs are pruned (run-dirs removed AND store rows trimmed) while within-N runs, any non-terminal run, and any UNPROJECTED run are KEPT. If wiring up portable projection in the harness is genuinely impractical, at minimum assert the enabled pass calls through to checkpointWal and removeRunDir for an eligible run (spy), and clearly note the projection setup limitation — but DO fully pin the disabled-inert case.
  - boot resilience: a binding that throws (e.g. a removeRunDir that throws) does not propagate out of runRetentionGcOnce (it swallows/logs), so boot is never blocked.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Both packages typecheck (`pnpm -C packages/core typecheck`, `pnpm -C packages/daemon typecheck`) and both suites pass (`pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run`, `pnpm -C packages/daemon exec vitest run`). Note: there is a KNOWN pre-existing order-flake in daemon mutations.test.ts ('leaves a ticket open when a ticket-fix run produces no verified commit') unrelated to retention — if you hit a single such failure, re-run the suite to confirm it is that flake and not your change.
- SHIP INERT verified: confirm in your write-up that with the default config the daemon boot path makes zero retention mutations. The live install's settings.json has NO retention key, so it loads the default (enabled:false) and the GC no-ops.
- Boot is never blocked by a GC error.

Return: the diff, the exact test + typecheck commands for both packages, and their passing output (with pass counts), and a one-line confirmation that disabled boot is inert.

data.reason: runRetentionGcOnce wired at boot after reconcileOrphans/migrate, gated on settings.retention.enabled (early-return → zero mutation when disabled, proven by inert test: run row + dir survive), binds real runsRoot/store/projectionCheckerFor/protectedRunIds(inFlight+stopControllers)/log rotation, whole body try/caught so a throwing store never blocks boot; enabled test proves projected-terminal-beyond-N pruned while unprojected/running/within-N kept; both typecheck 0, core 713/713, daemon 432/432.

data.task:
ATOM 8 (proof harness) of the machine-local cache retention priority — author a single-command, self-contained proof script `scripts/proof-retention.mjs` that exercises the SHIPPED retention engine end-to-end against a DISPOSABLE temp install and asserts every acceptance criterion. This is the runnable archive proof (replaces a manual founder checklist). It must touch ONLY temp dirs under the OS tmpdir — NEVER the real install `local/`.

RUNNABILITY: it must run via one documented command using the repo's existing TS runner (tsx), e.g. `pnpm -w exec tsx scripts/proof-retention.mjs` (confirm the exact incantation works in this repo and put it in a top-of-file comment). It imports the SHIPPED public surface from `@cocoder/core`: `openRunStore`, `runRetentionGc`, `projectionCheckerFor`, `removeLocalRunDir`, `rotateLogFile`, `resolveRetentionConfig` (and any types needed). For projection records, write the portable `cocoder/runs/<displayNumber>-<runId>/run.json` that `readPortableRunById` reads — use the portable writer if one is exported from @cocoder/core, otherwise hand-write the run.json matching the PortableRunFile shape so projectionCheckerFor resolves it. Build the deps object the same way the daemon wiring (`runRetentionGcOnce` in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts) does, so the proof matches production composition.

SAFETY (assert at runtime, fail hard otherwise): create the entire fixture under `mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cocoder-proof-retention-'))`; the file-backed store db, the runsRoot scratch dirs, and the fake repo (with cocoder/runs/) all live inside it. Before doing anything destructive, assert the temp root is under tmpdir() and is NOT equal to or a parent of the real repo's local/ (resolve paths and check). Remove the temp root at the end (even on failure). Use a file-backed db (join(tmpRoot,'cocoder.db')) NOT :memory: so the WAL checkpoint is meaningful.

SCENARIOS — drive the REAL `runRetentionGc` (enabled config via resolveRetentionConfig({enabled:true,keepLastNPerWorkspace:25})) and assert, printing a per-check PASS/FAIL line. Exit non-zero on ANY failure; exit 0 only if ALL pass, printing a final summary.
  1. BOUNDED: workspace A seeded with 30 projected, terminal (completed) runs — each with a runsRoot scratch dir AND a projected cocoder/runs record. After the pass: exactly the 25 newest remain (store rows + scratch dirs) and the 5 oldest are gone (rows + dirs). Print 'local runs dirs: 25 (was 30)'.
  2. IDEMPOTENT: run the pass a SECOND time → zero further prunes (prunedRunIds empty), footprint unchanged. (Acceptance: repeated GC never grows unbounded.)
  3. FAIRNESS: workspace B seeded with 3 runs — all 3 survive untouched regardless of A's churn (A's eviction never touches B).
  4. PENDING/IN-FLIGHT EXCLUSION: a `running` and an `awaiting-founder` run in A placed beyond rank 25 are NEVER pruned (rows + dirs survive). Also pass one of them in `protectedRunIds` and assert it appears in `skippedProtectedRunIds` and is kept.
  5. PROJECTION-GATING: a terminal run in A beyond rank 25 whose cocoder/runs record was NOT written is KEPT (durable record absent → never pruned).
  6. RECURRENCE SURVIVES: a fault-bearing terminal run in A beyond rank 25 (record a `fault-triaged` event) is pruned of its heavy rows but its run row + fault remain — assert `store.listFaultHistory('workspace-a')` still returns its fault AFTER the pass.
  7. WAL CHECKPOINT: the pass calls checkpointWal on the file-backed db without throwing (assert the returned result is an object of numbers).
  8. SURFACED, NOT SILENT: capture the deps.log output and assert it contains the formatted retention plan (per-workspace kept/total + the prune list) — proving no silent deletion.

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- RUN the harness yourself and paste its full PASS output as evidence; it must exit 0 with every check green.
- It must be deterministic (seed createdAt via explicit values, not wall-clock; vary per run) and leave NO temp dirs behind.
- Do NOT modify any engine/source file in this atom — the harness only consumes the shipped exports. If you find a real bug in the engine while writing the proof, STOP and report it rather than papering over it in the script.
- `pnpm -C packages/core typecheck` and `pnpm -C packages/daemon typecheck` still pass and both vitest suites still pass (the script is not a vitest test, but adding it must not break lint/typecheck config; if the repo typechecks scripts/, ensure the file passes — otherwise place/annotate it so it is not swept into a package tsconfig).

Return: the script diff, the exact command you ran, and its full stdout showing all checks PASS and exit 0.

data.reason: Ran scripts/proof-retention.mjs (pnpm -w exec tsx) — exit 0, all 31 checks PASS: safe-temp-root isolation, bounded 25-of-30, idempotent second pass, cross-workspace fairness, running/awaiting/protected/unprojected all kept, WAL numeric, log surfaces plan+prune list, and recurrence (fault row+history retained while heavy rows+dir pruned); consumes only shipped @cocoder/core exports, no engine source touched, temp root cleaned up.

data.task:
ATOM 9 (decision record) of the machine-local cache retention priority — record the now-implemented retention architecture as an ADR so the decision has a durable governed home (it currently lives only in the priority Playbook + the code). DOCS ONLY: do NOT change any engine/source file; this atom writes one ADR (and at most a one-line cross-reference in the existing local/ governance ADR). Apply correctness>clarity>elegance: terse, one owner, no restating code line-by-line.

FIRST READ: `cocoder/decisions/` — determine the NEXT ADR number and MATCH the exact existing ADR file format (frontmatter/sections/naming convention of the sibling ADRs; do not invent a new shape). Read `ADR-0027` (the machine-local `local/` cache governance ADR) since this new ADR is its child/extension. Also skim the priority Playbook for this priority (cocoder/priorities/) for the canonical wording of the Objective/Model so the ADR is consistent with it.

WRITE a new ADR capturing the DECISION (not a tutorial). It must state, concisely:
- DECISION: bound machine-local cache growth with a per-workspace 'keep last N runs' retention policy (default N=25), applied to BOTH the `local/runs/<workspaceId>/<runId>` scratch dirs AND the shared SQLite store rows. One policy, two mechanisms.
- REJECTED alternatives and why: time-based retention (punishes an infrequently-run repo — its good recent runs age out) and global last-N (an active workspace evicts an idle workspace's runs). Per-workspace keep-last-N is time-independent and fair; bounded total ≈ N × workspaces.
- SAFETY INVARIANTS (hard): GC a run's local state only AFTER its durable record is projected to the repo's `cocoder/runs/` (projection-gating); never prune a non-terminal/pending run (running, awaiting-founder, awaiting-archive-confirmation, held) or a run the daemon is actively driving (protectedRunIds); preserve cross-run fault recurrence — `listFaultHistory` reads the DB, so a pruned run that has `fault-triaged` events keeps its run row + those events while its heavy rows (sessions, work-items, commit-links, non-fault events) are trimmed; a fault-free pruned run is removed whole.
- HOUSEKEEPING: WAL checkpoint (TRUNCATE) and size-based rotation of `oz-audit.log` + `local/oz/` turn logs.
- ROLLOUT: ships INERT — config `retention.enabled` defaults false in `local/settings.json` (coercion owned by core `resolveRetentionConfig`); a single GC pass runs at daemon boot (after orphan reconcile + legacy-dir migration) and no-ops while disabled. Enabling it on a live install is a deliberate founder action after proving in isolation. Name the proof artifact: `scripts/proof-retention.mjs` (one command, exit 0 = all invariants hold against a disposable temp install).
- Reference the implementation owners by path WITHOUT duplicating logic: core `packages/core/src/runner/{retention,retention-plan,retention-gc,run-dir,log-rotation}.ts`, store `pruneRunRows`/`checkpointWal`, daemon `runRetentionGcOnce` (boot wiring) + `Settings.retention`.
- Link to ADR-0027 as the parent. Optionally add ONE cross-reference line in ADR-0027 pointing to this ADR (only if ADR-0027's format has a natural place for it; do not restructure ADR-0027).

ACCEPTANCE / WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- 'Just docs' can still be behavior-pinned: if the repo has any test that asserts ADR/decision structure, an ADR index, or decision frontmatter (search tests for 'decisions'/'ADR'/'adr'), RUN that suite and make the new ADR conform. At minimum run `pnpm -C packages/core exec vitest run` and `pnpm -C packages/daemon exec vitest run` plus both typechecks to confirm the docs addition breaks nothing, and any governance/docs suite that touches cocoder/decisions.
- No engine/source/test logic changes — only the ADR markdown (and an optional one-line ADR-0027 reference). Confirm via your own diff that only cocoder/decisions/** (and at most ADR-0027) changed.

Return: the diff, the next-ADR-number you chose and why, the exact commands you ran (including any decisions/governance suite), and their passing output.
